tasks.title: Clarify Atlas OS implementation choices and workflow constraints
tasks.description: Capture decisions on language/runtime for node agent and control plane, log storage backend, and minimum device support to align the build workflow with the PRD's phased plan.
tasks.details: Document decisions for: node agent language (Go/Python), control plane hosting model, logging storage (JSONL vs DB), minimum OS targets, and initial device roles. Record in a short decisions doc under atlas/docs or root-level notes.
tasks.testStrategy: Acceptance: decisions recorded and referenced by subsequent tasks.

subtasks.title: Research and document node agent language options
subtasks.description: Analyze and document trade-offs between Go and Python for the Atlas node agent, focusing on cross-platform support (Linux, macOS, Windows), resource efficiency (memory footprint, CPU usage), cold start time, binary distribution, ecosystem maturity for system monitoring/task execution, and development velocity. Consider implications for the AI logging wrapper (Task 7) and task execution API (Task 6).
subtasks.details: Details: Analyze and document trade-offs between Go and Python for the Atlas node agent, focusing on cross-platform support (Linux, macOS, Windows), resource efficiency (memory footprint, CPU usage), cold start time, binary distribution, ecosystem maturity for system monitoring/task execution, and development velocity. Consider implications for the AI logging wrapper (Task 7) and task execution API (Task 6).

subtasks.title: Define control plane hosting model architecture
subtasks.description: Specify the control plane architecture pattern, including hosting model (self-hosted vs cloud-optional), scaling approach (single-master vs HA), service discovery mechanism for multi-device federation (Task 10), and API surface (REST/gRPC). Address whether control plane runs on one designated device or separately.
subtasks.details: Details: Specify the control plane architecture pattern, including hosting model (self-hosted vs cloud-optional), scaling approach (single-master vs HA), service discovery mechanism for multi-device federation (Task 10), and API surface (REST/gRPC). Address whether control plane runs on one designated device or separately.

subtasks.title: Specify logging storage backend decision matrix
subtasks.description: Evaluate and document the logging storage backend choice between JSONL files and database solutions (SQLite, PostgreSQL, time-series DB) for AI interaction logs. Analyze write performance, query capabilities, storage growth projections, backup/sync complexity across devices, and maintenance overhead. Consider both single-device and federated scenarios (Task 10).
subtasks.details: Details: Evaluate and document the logging storage backend choice between JSONL files and database solutions (SQLite, PostgreSQL, time-series DB) for AI interaction logs. Analyze write performance, query capabilities, storage growth projections, backup/sync complexity across devices, and maintenance overhead. Consider both single-device and federated scenarios (Task 10).

subtasks.title: Define minimum device support matrix and initial device role taxonomy
subtasks.description: Document minimum OS/platform targets for Phase 1 (Linux distros, macOS versions, Windows support level) and define initial device role taxonomy (primary/secondary, compute/storage, agent host types). Specify bootstrap requirements and capability tag schema for task routing (Task 6).
subtasks.details: Details: Document minimum OS/platform targets for Phase 1 (Linux distros, macOS versions, Windows support level) and define initial device role taxonomy (primary/secondary, compute/storage, agent host types). Specify bootstrap requirements and capability tag schema for task routing (Task 6).

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break down into: 1) Research and document node agent language options (Go vs Python trade-offs for cross-platform support, resource efficiency, and ecosystem), 2) Define control plane hosting model architecture (centralized vs distributed, scaling considerations), 3) Specify logging storage backend decision matrix (JSONL vs database with performance/maintenance analysis), 4) Define minimum device support matrix and initial device role taxonomy

tasks.title: Node agent skeleton (per-device daemon/CLI)

tasks.details: Include device ID generation, capability discovery (OS/CPU/GPU), and a heartbeat loop. Keep filesystem roots read-only by default.
tasks.testStrategy: Start agent locally and confirm it emits a heartbeat payload and capabilities snapshot.

tasks.title: Context/decision graph prototype
tasks.description: Generate a basic context graph from logs to answer "what context was used" queries.
tasks.details: Implement a script that builds a simple graph (nodes: runs, files, devices; edges: used-in/produced-by).
tasks.testStrategy: Run the script and verify it produces a graph output file and simple query results.

subtasks.title: Design graph data model and entity schemas
subtasks.description: Define the complete graph data model including node types, edge types, and their attributes with JSON schema definitions
subtasks.details: Create schemas for nodes: Run (id, timestamp, device, model, context_size), File (path, hash, size, created_at), Device (id, hostname, role), Model (name, provider, version). Edge types: used_in (file→run), produced_by (file→run), preceded_by (run→run). Store schemas in atlas/config/graph-schemas.json. Include validation rules and examples.
subtasks.testStrategy: Validate schemas using jsonschema Python library against example entities; document schema in graph-model.md

subtasks.title: Implement JSONL log parser for graph entity extraction
subtasks.description: Build a parser that reads atlas/logs/*.jsonl files and extracts graph entities and relationships
subtasks.details: Create atlas/services/log_parser.py with functions: parse_log_file(filepath) returns List[Entity], extract_relationships(events) returns List[Edge]. Handle log formats: context_used, file_created, run_completed. Map log event fields to graph schema attributes. Handle malformed entries gracefully with error logging. Output structured data ready for graph construction.
subtasks.testStrategy: Create sample JSONL log file with 10-20 events; verify parser extracts correct entities and relationships; unit test with pytest

subtasks.title: Implement graph storage and query engine
subtasks.description: Build graph representation using NetworkX with file-based persistence and query API
subtasks.details: Create atlas/services/context_graph.py: class ContextGraph with methods add_node(), add_edge(), save_graph(path), load_graph(path). Use NetworkX MultiDiGraph for directed multi-edges. Implement query methods: get_context_for_run(run_id), get_produced_files(run_id), get_run_history(file_path). Store graph as .graphml and nodes/edges as .jsonl for portability. Add CLI interface: atlas-query --context <run_id>.
subtasks.testStrategy: Load sample data, verify persistence (save/load cycle), test all query methods against known graph state

subtasks.title: Create graph visualization and export functionality
subtasks.description: Implement multiple export formats for graph visualization including GraphViz DOT, JSON, and ASCII
subtasks.details: Add export methods to ContextGraph: export_dot(filepath) generates GraphViz DOT with subgraphs for each entity type, color-coded edges; export_json(filepath) dumps nodes/edges as structured JSON; export_ascii(filepath) creates simple text visualization. Create atlas/services/graph_viz.py with layout helpers. Add CLI: atlas-export --format dot|json|ascii --output <path>. Include sample generated outputs in atlas/docs/graph-examples/
subtasks.testStrategy: Generate all three formats from sample graph; verify DOT renders with dot/xdot; validate JSON schema; check ASCII readability

subtasks.title: Create integration test suite and documentation
subtasks.description: Build end-to-end tests and documentation for the context graph system
subtasks.details: Create tests/test_context_graph.py with: test_full_pipeline() (parse→build→query→export), test_query_accuracy() (verify context chains), test_large_graph() (1000+ nodes performance). Write atlas/docs/context-graph.md covering: architecture, data model, usage examples, query patterns, troubleshooting. Add quickstart example: atlas/scripts/build_demo_graph.sh generates sample graph from synthetic logs. Include integration with existing atlas logging if available.
subtasks.testStrategy: Run full test suite with pytest -v; verify documentation examples execute successfully; demo script produces valid output

tasks.expansionPrompt: Break into: 1) Design graph data model (nodes: runs, files, devices, models; edges: used-in, produced-by, preceded-by), 2) Implement log parser to extract graph entities and relationships from JSONL logs, 3) Choose graph representation (adjacency list, networkx, or custom) and storage format, 4) Build graph query API (what context was used for X? what did file Y produce?), 5) Create visualization export (graphviz DOT, JSON, or simple ASCII)
